evals.prompt: VIX is at 28, up from 18 three weeks ago. SPY IV Rank is 72nd percentile. The vol term structure is in backwardation (VIX3M/VIX = 0.92). SPY's 20-day HV is 19%. What does the quant signal dashboard say, and what credit spread does it recommend?
evals.expected_output: Complete quant signal dashboard with IV vs HV analysis, skew assessment, Kelly sizing, and a specific credit spread recommendation
expectations: Calculates IV vs HV spread (IV 28% vs HV 19% = IV premium)
Interprets IV Rank 72nd percentile as elevated (sell signal context)
Flags backwardation (VIX3M/VIX < 1.0) as a caution signal against aggressive short vol
Recommends a credit spread (Bull Put or Bear Call) with the IV premium rationale
Includes model skepticism: questions whether IV rank is reliable if regime has changed
Includes Devil's Advocate: IV high may be justified if real risks exist

evals.prompt: GLD's IV is 14%, its 52-week IV range is 10%-22%, making IV Rank at 40th percentile. The 20-day HV is 12%. SKEW index is 108. VIX is 18. What is the quant read on GLD, and how should it be sized?
evals.expected_output: GLD-specific quant analysis with IV/HV comparison, Kelly calculation, and credit spread recommendation
expectations: Calculates IV Rank (40th percentile = moderate, neither high nor low)
Compares IV 14% vs HV 12% = IV slightly elevated = mild sell signal
Notes SKEW 108 as near-neutral (mild put demand)
Calculates Kelly fraction for low-vol environment (VIX 18 → 1.0x multiplier)
Recommends appropriate position size relative to {{YOUR_CAPITAL}}
Recommends Bull Put Spread as the credit spread structure
Includes data freshness note and source verification requirement
